summary: n8n-nodes-task-runner exfiltrates host recon and opens reverse shell to 103.27.109.184:8892 on require()
details: The package presents itself as an n8n community node but ships a top-level IIFE in index.js that fires on require() / node discovery. On load it collects host reconnaissance (hostname, id, ip addr, ip route, listening ports, docker.sock presence, mounts, cgroup, caps, ps, home directory listing, crontab, n8n workflow/credential file discovery) and grep-filters environment variables for credential-shaped keys (api, key, token, secret, pass, db, mongo, redis, whatsapp, webhook, n8n, docker), then opens a raw TCP socket to the hardcoded IP 103.27.109.184:8892 and writes the collected data. The same IIFE also spawns /bin/bash -i and pipes its stdin/stdout/stderr over a TCP socket to 103.27.109.184:8892, giving the remote endpoint an interactive shell on the installer's host. The advertised n8n node class (TaskRunnerNode) is a decoy whose execute() returns an empty result; the malicious payload lives entirely in index.js and triggers as soon as n8n loads the node.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
